notes: Could not reproduce DllNotFoundException: libSkiaSharp on macOS arm64 with .NET SDK 10.0.102 and wasm-tools workload installed. Tested SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor 3.116.1 on net10.0 Blazor WASM — both with and without explicit WasmBuildNative=true. Both configurations built successfully with native WASM compilation and rendered correctly in the browser (cornflower blue canvas with text, SUCCESS logged to console, zero errors). This is consistent with the related repro for #3422 which also could not reproduce. The issue is likely environment-specific — possibly caused by a missing or mismatched wasm-tools workload version on the reporter's machine.

versionResults.notes: Build succeeded with native WASM compilation. App rendered SKCanvasView correctly in browser. No DllNotFoundException or other errors in browser console.

reproductionSteps.description: Create Blazor WASM project targeting net10.0
reproductionSteps.command: cd /tmp/repro-3435 && dotnet new blazorwasm -n Repro --framework net10.0

reproductionSteps.description: Add WasmBuildNative=true to csproj and create repro Razor page with SKCanvasView drawing code

filesCreated.description: Blazor page with SKCanvasView that draws cornflower blue background and white text

reproductionSteps.description: Build Blazor WASM project with native WASM compilation (WasmBuildNative=true)
reproductionSteps.output:
Build succeeded with 2 warning(s) in 8.5s
Warnings: SKPaint.TextSize and SKCanvas.DrawText obsolete (use SKFont instead)

reproductionSteps.description: Serve Blazor app and verify in browser — check for DllNotFoundException in console
reproductionSteps.command: dotnet run --urls http://localhost:5111 --no-build && navigate browser to http://localhost:5111
reproductionSteps.output: Browser console: SUCCESS: rendered 1600x1200 (logged twice). Zero errors. Page rendered SKCanvasView with cornflower blue background and white 'Hello SkiaSharp!' text. No DllNotFoundException or TypeInitializationException.

reproductionSteps.description: Re-test WITHOUT explicit WasmBuildNative=true (fresh project) to check if SkiaSharp package targets auto-enable it
reproductionSteps.command: cd /tmp/repro-3435-nowasmflag/Repro && dotnet build && dotnet run --urls http://localhost:5112
reproductionSteps.output: Build succeeded with 2 warning(s) in 6.7s. Browser console: SUCCESS: rendered 1600x1200. Zero errors. Native WASM compilation was automatically triggered by package targets.

corrections.corrected: Issue is WASM/Blazor, not Windows-specific. Reporter explicitly says 'Platform: Blazor, WASM'. Correct label should be os/WASM.

actionability.reason: Cannot reproduce on .NET SDK 10.0.102 with wasm-tools installed. This is the same symptom as #3422 (DllNotFoundException: libSkiaSharp in Blazor WASM on .NET 10), which was also not-reproduced. A community member already identified this as a duplicate. The root cause is likely missing wasm-tools workload.

workarounds: Ensure wasm-tools workload is installed: dotnet workload install wasm-tools
Update to the latest .NET 10 SDK (10.0.102+) and run dotnet workload restore
Track updates in #3422 which has broader investigation

proposedResponse.summary: Not reproduced — likely missing wasm-tools workload. Duplicate of #3422.
proposedResponse.body:
Thanks for the report. We attempted to reproduce this on macOS arm64 with .NET SDK 10.0.102 and SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor 3.116.1 targeting `net10.0`, but were unable to observe the `DllNotFoundException: libSkiaSharp` error.

The Blazor WASM app built successfully with native WASM compilation and rendered correctly in the browser — both with and without explicit `WasmBuildNative=true` in the `.csproj`.

This appears to be the same issue tracked in #3422. The most common cause is a missing or mismatched `wasm-tools` workload. Could you try the following?

1. Update to the latest .NET 10 SDK
2. Run `dotnet workload install wasm-tools` (or `dotnet workload restore`)
3. Clean rebuild: `rm -rf bin/ obj/` then `dotnet build`

If the issue persists, could you share:
- Output of `dotnet --info` (run **outside** any repo with `global.json`)
- Output of `dotnet workload list`
- Your exact `.csproj` file

Closing as duplicate of #3422 to keep investigation centralized.

actions.description: Fix platform labels — this is WASM, not Windows

missingInfo: Output of 'dotnet --info' run outside repos with global.json
